title: Why we use 4 button variants
decision: We standardized on 4 button variants: primary, secondary, destructive, and ghost
context: Previously had 8+ button styles causing inconsistency and decision paralysis
reasoning: Primary: One main action per screen section
Destructive: Delete, remove, or dangerous actions
alternatives: Material Design (text, outlined, contained) - too many options
Single button style with size variations - not enough hierarchy
author: Design System Team

title: Form validation pattern
decision: All forms use inline validation with specific error messages
context: Forms showed only generic error messages at the top
reasoning: Inline errors appear next to the field causing the issue
Error messages explain what's wrong and how to fix it
Validation runs onBlur (not onChange) to avoid interrupting typing
Success indicators shown after field is valid
implementation: Use aria-describedby to link error messages
Show errors in red with icon
Provide specific guidance: 'Email must include @' not 'Invalid input'

examples.good: Password must be at least 8 characters

author: UX Team

title: Spacing rhythm guidelines
decision: Use 4px base unit for all spacing (4, 8, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64)
reasoning: Works well at all screen sizes
doNot: Don't use arbitrary values like 13px or 27px
Don't use spacing tokens for sizing (use sizing tokens)
Don't break the rhythm without design team approval
author: Design System Team

title: When should I use a Card component?
question: When should I use a Card component vs just a div?
answer: Use Card when you need to visually group related content and make it interactive or scannable
guidance: ✅ Use Card for: Product listings, feature highlights, team member profiles, article previews
✅ Card benefits: Built-in elevation, padding, hover states, accessibility
❌ Don't use Card for: Page sections, navigation items, form containers
❌ Instead use: Section tags, nav elements, fieldsets

examples.recommendation: Use Card - each product is a discrete, clickable unit
Use section tag - not a discrete content unit

relatedQuestions: What's the difference between Card and Panel?
author: Design System Team
